title: GitHub Review by greptile-apps[bot]
summary: The reviewer wants four changes: (1) add a `+kubebuilder:validation:XValidation` CEL rule on the `Cache` field to enforce mutual exclusivity of `clusterRef` vs `servers` (matching the existing pattern on `Database`), plus a corresponding defense-in-depth check in the webhook's `validate()` function; (2) fix a test (`TestValidate_MaxActiveKeysZeroAllowed`) that wraps its assertion in an `if err != nil` guard, making it pass vacuously — replace with an unconditional `g.Expect(err).NotTo(HaveOccurred())`; (3) add a TODO comment in `main.go` noting that `SetupWebhookWithManager` isn't wired up yet, tracking it to CC-0012.

code_comments.body:
**Missing XOR XValidation for `cache` field**

`KeystoneSpec.Database` carries an explicit XOR constraint:

```go
// +kubebuilder:validation:XValidation:rule="has(self.clusterRef) != has(self.host)",message="exactly one of clusterRef or host must be set"
Database commonv1.DatabaseSpec `json:"database"`
```

`CacheSpec` is documented with the same mutual-exclusivity intent (`types.go`: `// Exactly one of ClusterRef or Servers must be set.`) and the field comment here also says *"Supports managed (clusterRef) and brownfield (servers) modes"*, but there is no equivalent XValidation rule on `Cache`. Without it, a user can supply both `clusterRef` **and** `servers` simultaneously, yielding an ambiguous configuration that the reconciler will have to handle without CRD-layer guidance.

```suggestion
	// Cache defines the Memcached cache configuration.
	// Supports managed (clusterRef) and brownfield (servers) modes.
	// +kubebuilder:validation:XValidation:rule="has(self.clusterRef) != has(self.servers)",message="exactly one of clusterRef or servers must be set"
	Cache commonv1.CacheSpec `json:"cache"`
```

After adding the marker, re-run `make manifests` to regenerate the CRD YAML with the corresponding `x-kubernetes-validations` entry.

Without it, a user can supply both `clusterRef` **and** `servers` simultaneously, yielding an ambiguous configuration that the reconciler will have to handle without CRD-layer guidance.

```suggestion

	// Supports managed (clusterRef) and brownfield (servers) modes.

	Cache commonv1.CacheSpec `json:"cache"`
```

After adding the marker, re-run `make manifests` to regenerate the CRD YAML with the corresponding `x-kubernetes-validations` entry.

How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise.

</details>
code_comments.author: greptile-apps[bot]
code_comments.resolution: Added XValidation CEL marker `rule="has(self.clusterRef) != has(self.servers)"` on the Cache field at keystone_types.go:53, exactly matching the suggested annotation to enforce mutual exclusivity at the CRD schema level.

code_comments.body:
**Conditional assertion silently skips on success**

The comment says `MaxActiveKeys = 0` is "allowed", but the assertion is wrapped in an `if err != nil` guard. If `ValidateCreate` returns `nil` (which it should for `validKeystone()` with only `MaxActiveKeys` changed to `0`), the body of the `if` is never evaluated and the test passes vacuously — it doesn't prove anything either way.

Given that `validKeystone()` supplies a fully-valid spec (`RotationSchedule: "0 0 * * 0"`, valid replicas, no plugins, no policyOverrides), the comment *"cron error may appear since we use validKeystone"* is also incorrect.

Replace the conditional guard with an unconditional assertion that zero is accepted without error:

```suggestion
func TestValidate_MaxActiveKeysZeroAllowed(t *testing.T) {
	g := NewGomegaWithT(t)
	w := &KeystoneWebhook{}
	k := validKeystone()
	// Zero is allowed because Default() converts 0 → 3 before validate() runs.
	// If validate() is called outside the normal admission path with 0,
	// it should not conflict with the defaulting logic.
	k.Spec.Fernet.MaxActiveKeys = 0

	_, err := w.ValidateCreate(context.Background(), k)
	g.Expect(err).NotTo(HaveOccurred())
}
```

code_comments.author: greptile-apps[bot]
code_comments.resolution: Replaced the conditional `if err != nil` guard with an unconditional `g.Expect(err).NotTo(HaveOccurred())` assertion in TestValidate_MaxActiveKeysZeroAllowed (keystone_webhook_test.go:166-177). Updated the comment to accurately explain why zero is allowed (Default() converts 0→3) and removed the incorrect note about cron errors.

code_comments.body:
**`CacheSpec` mutual-exclusivity constraint is unenforced**

`DatabaseSpec` has an XValidation CEL rule enforcing `clusterRef XOR host`:
```go
// +kubebuilder:validation:XValidation:rule="has(self.clusterRef) != has(self.host)",message="exactly one of clusterRef or host must be set"
Database commonv1.DatabaseSpec `json:"database"`
```

`CacheSpec` carries the same design intent — the `types.go` comment reads *"Exactly one of ClusterRef or Servers must be set"* — but neither the CRD schema nor the webhook's `validate()` enforces this. A user can therefore submit a `Keystone` with both `cache.clusterRef` and `cache.servers` set, or with neither set (only `backend`). The webhook has no corresponding check, so even with the admission webhook active there is no defence-in-depth here.

Add the matching XValidation marker:

```suggestion
	// Cache defines the Memcached cache configuration.
	// Supports managed (clusterRef) and brownfield (servers) modes.
	// +kubebuilder:validation:XValidation:rule="has(self.clusterRef) != (self.servers != null && size(self.servers) > 0)",message="exactly one of clusterRef or servers must be set"
	Cache commonv1.CacheSpec `json:"cache"`
```

A corresponding defence-in-depth check should also be added to the `validate()` function in `keystone_webhook.go`, mirroring the pattern used for `database`.

<details><summary>Prompt To Fix With AI</summary>

`````markdown

Line: 51-53

```go

Database commonv1.DatabaseSpec `json:"database"`
```

`CacheSpec` carries the same design intent — the `types.go` comment reads *"Exactly one of ClusterRef or Servers must be set"* — but neither the CRD schema nor the webhook's `validate()` enforces this. A user can therefore submit a `Keystone` with both `cache.clusterRef` and `cache.servers` set, or with neither set (only `backend`). The webhook has no corresponding check, so even with the admission webhook active there is no defence-in-depth here.

Add the matching XValidation marker:

```suggestion
	// Cache defines the Memcached cache configuration.
	// Supports managed (clusterRef) and brownfield (servers) modes.
	// +kubebuilder:validation:XValidation:rule="has(self.clusterRef) != (self.servers != null && size(self.servers) > 0)",message="exactly one of clusterRef or servers must be set"
	Cache commonv1.CacheSpec `json:"cache"`
```

A corresponding defence-in-depth check should also be added to the `validate()` function in `keystone_webhook.go`, mirroring the pattern used for `database`.

How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise.
`````
</details>
code_comments.author: greptile-apps[bot]
code_comments.resolution: Added both the XValidation CEL rule on the Cache field (same as comment [0]) and a defence-in-depth check in the webhook's validate() function (keystone_webhook.go:102-112) that rejects specs where `hasCacheClusterRef == hasCacheServers` (i.e., both set or neither set). Four new test cases were added to keystone_webhook_test.go (lines 248-293) covering servers-only, clusterRef-only, both-rejected, and neither-rejected scenarios.

code_comments.body:
**Webhook not wired up in `main.go`**

`SetupWebhookWithManager` is defined but never called. Checking `operators/keystone/main.go`, it only registers the scheme:
```go
utilruntime.Must(keystonev1alpha1.AddToScheme(scheme))
```

Until `SetupWebhookWithManager` is invoked (typically alongside the controller's `SetupWithManager` call), **all webhook validation is inactive**. The only admission control in effect is the CRD XValidation CEL rules. This means:

- `cron.ParseStandard` validation for `RotationSchedule` won't run
- Duplicate `configSection` detection in `Plugins` won't run
- `PolicyOverrides` source requirement (REQ-004) won't run

The PR description notes that reconciler wiring is deferred to CC-0012+, so leaving it unregistered is understandable as a scaffold. A tracking comment in `main.go` near the `// +kubebuilder:scaffold:scheme` marker would clarify that the webhook registration is pending:

```go
// TODO(CC-0012): Call (&keystonev1alpha1.KeystoneWebhook{}).SetupWebhookWithManager(mgr)
// once the Keystone controller is wired up.
```

<details><summary>Prompt To Fix With AI</summary>

`````markdown
This is a comment left during a code review.
Path: operators/keystone/api/v1alpha1/keystone_webhook.go
Line: 31-36

Comment:
**Webhook not wired up in `main.go`**

`SetupWebhookWithManager` is defined but never called. Checking `operators/keystone/main.go`, it only registers the scheme:
```go
utilruntime.Must(keystonev1alpha1.AddToScheme(scheme))
```

Until `SetupWebhookWithManager` is invoked (typically alongside the controller's `SetupWithManager` call), **all webhook validation is inactive**. The only admission control in effect is the CRD XValidation CEL rules. This means:

- `cron.ParseStandard` validation for `RotationSchedule` won't run
- Duplicate `configSection` detection in `Plugins` won't run
- `PolicyOverrides` source requirement (REQ-004) won't run

The PR description notes that reconciler wiring is deferred to CC-0012+, so leaving it unregistered is understandable as a scaffold. A tracking comment in `main.go` near the `// +kubebuilder:scaffold:scheme` marker would clarify that the webhook registration is pending:

```go
// TODO(CC-0012): Call (&keystonev1alpha1.KeystoneWebhook{}).SetupWebhookWithManager(mgr)
// once the Keystone controller is wired up.
```

How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise.
`````
</details>
code_comments.author: greptile-apps[bot]
code_comments.resolution: Added `// TODO(CC-0012): Call (&keystonev1alpha1.KeystoneWebhook{}).SetupWebhookWithManager(mgr)` tracking comment in main.go:36-37 inside the SetupFunc, clarifying that webhook registration is intentionally deferred until the Keystone controller is wired up in CC-0012.

resolution_summary: All four review comments were addressed: XValidation CEL rule added for cache mutual-exclusivity on the CRD field, defence-in-depth webhook validation added in validate() for cache clusterRef/servers XOR check, the MaxActiveKeysZeroAllowed test was rewritten with an unconditional assertion replacing the vacuous if-guard, and a TODO(CC-0012) tracking comment was added in main.go for deferred webhook registration. Four new cache validation tests (TestValidate_CacheWithServersOnly, CacheWithClusterRefOnly, CacheBothClusterRefAndServersRejected, CacheNeitherClusterRefNorServersRejected) were added in keystone_webhook_test.go to cover the new defence-in-depth webhook check from comment [2].
